src: πλὴν ἀκούσατε τρέμοντες δι᾽ ὃν ἐτρόμαξεν ἡ γῆ.
tok: pres act ptcp nom pl m
lit: But hear, trembling, because of whom the earth trembled.
sm: But listen in trembling to the one because of whom the earth trembled.

syn.sub: Relative clause δι᾽ ὃν ἐτρόμαξεν ἡ γῆ modifies the implied object of ἀκούσατε.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: ἀκούσατε. Participial modifier: τρέμοντες. Relative clause verb: ἐτρόμαξεν with subject ἡ γῆ.
syn.ptc: τρέμοντες is circumstantial, describing the manner in which the audience is to hear.
syn.rhet: The line shifts from accusation to awe, preparing the paradoxes that follow.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one who hung the earth is hanged.
sm: The one who hung the earth now hangs.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article. Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to period punctuation.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κρεμάσας τὴν γῆν. Verb: κρέμαται.
syn.ptc: The aorist participle is substantival and antecedent to the finite verb.
syn.rhet: A creation act is inverted into crucifixion by stark paradox.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one who fixed the heavens has been fixed fast.
sm: The one who fixed the heavens has himself been fixed fast.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to period punctuation.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ πήξας τοὺς οὐρανούς. Verb: πέπηκται.
syn.ptc: The substantival participle recalls the creator's act, while the perfect finite verb states the present humiliation.
syn.rhet: The line balances creative stability with the immobilization of crucifixion.

src: ὅ στηρίξας τὰ πάντα ἐπὶ ξύλου ἐστήρικται"
ana: ὁ στηρίξας τὰ πάντα ἐπὶ ξύλου ἐστήρικται.
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
has been fixed; has been fastened
lit: The one who established all things has been fastened on wood.
sm: The one who made all things firm has been fastened to the tree.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὅ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article. Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to period punctuation.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ στηρίξας τὰ πάντα. Verb: ἐστήρικται. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ ξύλου modifies the finite verb.
syn.ptc: The participle is substantival and antecedent, naming the prior cosmic act.
syn.rhet: The universal stabilizer is himself fixed upon the cross.

lit: The master has been outraged.
sm: The Master has been outraged.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to period punctuation.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ δεσπότης. Verb: ὕβρισται.
syn.rhet: The clause strips the paradox down to a bare, accusatory statement.

lit: God has been murdered.
sm: God has been murdered.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὅ to ὁ; the substantival subject requires the article. Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to period punctuation.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ θεός. Verb: πεφόνευται.
syn.rhet: The line is the blunt theological climax of the paradox series.

src: ὃ βασιλεὺς τοῦ ᾿Ισραὴλ ἀνήρηται ὑπὸ δεξιᾶς ᾿Ισραηλίτιδος.
ana: ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῦ Ἰσραὴλ ἀνήρηται ὑπὸ δεξιᾶς Ἰσραηλίτιδος.
tok: has been slain; has been done away with
lit: The king of Israel has been slain by the right hand of an Israelite.
sm: The king of Israel has been slain by an Israelite right hand.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ and removed stray spacing psili from ᾿Ισραὴλ and ᾿Ισραηλίτιδος.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῦ Ἰσραήλ. Verb: ἀνήρηται. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ δεξιᾶς Ἰσραηλίτιδος.
syn.rhet: The paradox series ends by locating the crime inside Israel itself.

src: ὦ φόνου καινοῦ, ὦ ἀδικίας καινῆς"
ana: ὦ φόνου καινοῦ, ὦ ἀδικίας καινῆς·
lit: O of new murder, O of new injustice;
sm: O unheard-of murder, O unheard-of injustice!
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as raised-dot punctuation to carry the exclamatory transition forward.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb; two vocative-like exclamatory genitive phrases stand in parallel.
syn.rhet: A double exclamation intensifies the lament before the next descriptive sentence.

src: ὁ δεσπότης παρεσχημάτισται γυμνῷ τῷ σώματι,
tok: has been exposed; has been disfigured in appearance
lit: The master has been exposed in naked body,
sm: The Master has been exposed with his body laid bare,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 772 adds a result/purpose clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ δεσπότης. Verb: παρεσχημάτισται. Dative of respect/state: γυμνῷ τῷ σώματι.
syn.rhet: The focus narrows from cosmic paradox to the shame of the crucified body.

src: καὶ οὐδὲ περιβολῆς ἠξίωται ἵνα μὴ θεαθῇ.
lit: And he has not even been deemed worthy of a covering so that he might not be seen.
sm: and he was not even granted a covering to spare him public exposure.

syn.sub: ἵνα μὴ θεαθῇ is a purpose/result clause dependent on ἠξίωται.
syn.svo: Subject: implicit ὁ δεσπότης from line 771. Verb: ἠξίωται. Genitive complement: περιβολῆς.
syn.rhet: The indignity is sharpened by denying even the minimal mercy of a covering.

lit: Because of this the luminaries turned away
sm: Because of this the heavenly lights turned away,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 774 continues the coordinated reaction.
syn.svo: Causal phrase: διὰ τοῦτο. Subject: οἱ φωστῆρες. Verb: ἀπεστράφησαν.
syn.rhet: Creation recoils in sympathy at the outrage.

lit: And the day darkened,
sm: and the day grew dark,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: removed stray digit 5 after the comma.

syn.sub: Line 775 gives the purpose of the darkening.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ἡμέρα. Verb: συνεσκότασεν.
syn.rhet: The reaction of the cosmos moves from stars to daylight itself.

src: ὅπως κρύψῃ τὸν ἐπὶ ξύλου γεγυμνωμένον,
tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: So that it might hide the one stripped naked on the wood,
sm: so that it might hide the one stripped naked upon the tree,

syn.sub: ὅπως κρύψῃ introduces the purpose of the day's darkening.
syn.svo: Subject of κρύψῃ: implicit ἡ ἡμέρα. Object: τὸν ... γεγυμνωμένον. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ ξύλου.
syn.ptc: γεγυμνωμένον is attributive to the hidden figure.
syn.rhet: Darkness becomes an act of reverent concealment.

src: οὐ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου σῶμα σκοτίζων
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Not darkening the body of the Lord
sm: not by darkening the Lord's body

syn.mc: Participial phrase dependent on the implicit subject of κρύψῃ in line 775.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; line 777 completes the contrast.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: ἡ ἡμέρα. Participle: σκοτίζων. Object: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου σῶμα.
syn.ptc: σκοτίζων is circumstantial, explaining what the darkening does not target.
syn.rhet: A negated participle prepares the antithesis between Christ and human sight.

lit: But the eyes of men.
sm: but by darkening the eyes of men.

syn.mc: Adversative object phrase completing the participial contrast of line 776.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: The object governed by σκοτίζων is supplied from line 776: τοὺς τῶν ἀνθρώπων ὀφθαλμούς.
syn.rhet: The contrast shifts darkness from Christ's body to human perception.

src: καὶ γὰρ τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ τρέμοντος ἐτρόμαξεν ἡ γῆ“
ana: καὶ γὰρ τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ τρέμοντος ἐτρόμαξεν ἡ γῆ·
tok: pres act ptcp gen sg m
lit: For, the people not trembling, the earth trembled;
sm: For while the people did not tremble, the earth trembled;
emd: Normalized the trailing OCR quotation mark as raised-dot punctuation.

syn.sub: Genitive absolute: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ τρέμοντος.
syn.svo: Main clause subject: ἡ γῆ. Verb: ἐτρόμαξεν.
syn.ptc: τρέμοντος in the genitive absolute sets human failure against cosmic response.
syn.rhet: The paradox now contrasts human insensibility with creation's sympathy.

src: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ φοβηθέντος ἐφοβήθησαν οἱ οὐρανοί"
ana: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ φοβηθέντος ἐφοβήθησαν οἱ οὐρανοί·
tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg m
lit: The people not having been afraid, the heavens were afraid;
sm: When the people were not afraid, the heavens became afraid;
emd: Normalized the trailing OCR quotation mark as raised-dot punctuation.

syn.sub: Genitive absolute: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ φοβηθέντος.
syn.svo: Main clause subject: οἱ οὐρανοί. Verb: ἐφοβήθησαν.
syn.ptc: φοβηθέντος in the genitive absolute contrasts Israel's failure with heaven's response.
syn.rhet: The parallelism escalates from earth to heavens.

src: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ περιεσχισμένου περιεσχίσατο ὁ ἄγγελος ἡ
ana: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ περιεσχισμένου περιεσχίσατο ὁ ἄγγελος·
tok: pf mp ptcp gen sg m
lit: The people not having rent [their garments], the angel rent [his garments];
sm: While the people did not rend their garments, the angel rent his.
emd: Followed change_log.txt by normalizing the stray final ἡ to raised-dot punctuation. External witnesses preserve περιεσχίσατο ὁ ἄγγελος; the middle is best taken as an implicit mourning gesture, with the object 'garments' understood.

syn.sub: Genitive absolute: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ περιεσχισμένου.
syn.svo: Main clause subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Verb: περιεσχίσατο. The implied object is something like τὰ ἱμάτια.
syn.ptc: περιεσχισμένου in the genitive absolute extends the series of human non-response.
syn.rhet: The pattern of cosmic and superhuman response continues: when Israel does not mourn, even the angel performs mourning.

src: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ κωκύσαντος ἐβρόντησεν ἐξ οὐρανοῦ κύριος
tok: aor act ptcp gen sg m
lit: The people not having wailed, the Lord thundered from heaven
sm: While the people did not wail, the Lord thundered from heaven

syn.mc: Main clause with genitive absolute, continued in line 782.
syn.sub: Genitive absolute: τοῦ λαοῦ μὴ κωκύσαντος.
syn.svo: Main clause subject: κύριος. Verb: ἐβρόντησεν. Source phrase: ἐξ οὐρανοῦ.
syn.ptc: κωκύσαντος in the genitive absolute continues the chain of Israel's failures.
syn.rhet: Israel's silence is answered by divine thunder.

lit: And the Most High gave voice.
sm: and the Most High uttered his voice.

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause completing the sentence begun in line 781.
syn.sub: No additional subordinate clause; the genitive absolute from line 781 still frames the scene.
syn.svo: Subject: ὕψιστος. Verb: ἔδωικεν. Object: φωνήν.
syn.rhet: The line closes the crescendo with the divine voice itself.

lit: Because of this, O Israel,
sm: For this reason, O Israel,
emd: Removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ισραήλ.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the main accusatory clauses follow in lines 784, 787, 789, and 790.
syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; vocative address prepares the indictment.
syn.rhet: The address pivots from cosmic witness back to direct accusation.

lit: At the Lord you did not tremble,
sm: Before the Lord you did not tremble,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; it is the first member of a parallel indictment continued in lines 787, 789, and contrasted in 790.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τοῦ κυρίου. Verb: ἐτρόμαξας. Subject: implied σύ / Israel.
syn.rhet: First negative member in the climactic second-person triad.

lit: At the Lord you were not afraid,
sm: Before the Lord you felt no fear,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; second member of the parallel indictment.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τοῦ κυρίου. Verb: ἐφοβήθης. Subject: implied σύ / Israel.
syn.rhet: The second negated clause echoes and intensifies line 784.

lit: Over the Lord you did not wail,
sm: You did not lament for the Lord,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; third member of the parallel indictment.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τοῦ κυρίου. Verb: ἐκώκυσας. Subject: implied σύ / Israel.
syn.rhet: The triad moves from fear to lament, preparing the final contrast.

lit: Over your firstborn you wailed.
sm: Yet you did wail over your own firstborn.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to period punctuation.

syn.mc: Main contrastive clause concluding the indictment.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῶν πρωτοτόκων σου. Verb: ἐκώκυσας. Subject: implied σύ / Israel.
syn.rhet: The accusation culminates in antithesis: no lament for the Lord, lament for one's own firstborn.
